title: Merge node SQL mode allows arbitrary file write (incomplete alasql file-access hardening, INTO handlers not disabled)
description: The Merge node's combineBySql mode runs a user-supplied SQL query through the alasql in-memory engine. Before executing the query the node calls disableAlasqlFileAccess(), which is intended to neutralise alasql's filesystem features, but the allow/deny list is incomplete: it overrides only a fixed subset of alasql.fn/alasql.from/alasql.engines handlers and never touches the alasql.into.* write handlers (FILE, TXT, CSV, JSON, XLS, XLSX, etc.) or the alasql.utils.saveFile/loadFile helpers. As a result a workflow author can run a query such as `SELECT ... INTO TXT('/path/to/file') FROM input1` and have alasql write attacker-chosen content to an attacker-chosen path on the n8n server's filesystem, leading to arbitrary file write and potential remote code execution. CVSS 9.4 (CVE-2026-25056, CWE-434/CWE-693).
